{"content": "     1|---
     2|sidebar_position: 2
     3|title: "Configuration"
     4|description: "Configure Hermes Agent — config.yaml, providers, models, API keys, and more"
     5|---
     6|
     7|# Configuration
     8|
     9|All settings are stored in the `~/.hermes/` directory for easy access.
    10|
    11|## Directory Structure
    12|
    13|```text
    14|~/.hermes/
    15|├── config.yaml     # Settings (model, terminal, TTS, compression, etc.)
    16|├── .env            # API keys and secrets
    17|├── auth.json       # OAuth provider credentials (Nous Portal, etc.)
    18|├── SOUL.md         # Primary agent identity (slot #1 in system prompt)
    19|├── memories/       # Persistent memory (MEMORY.md, USER.md)
    20|├── skills/         # Agent-created skills (managed via skill_manage tool)
    21|├── cron/           # Scheduled jobs
    22|├── sessions/       # Gateway sessions
    23|└── logs/           # Logs (errors.log, gateway.log — secrets auto-redacted)
    24|```
    25|
    26|## Managing Configuration
    27|
    28|```bash
    29|hermes config              # View current configuration
    30|hermes config edit         # Open config.yaml in your editor
    31|hermes config set KEY VAL  # Set a specific value
    32|hermes config check        # Check for missing options (after updates)
    33|hermes config migrate      # Interactively add missing options
    34|
    35|# Examples:
    36|hermes config set model anthropic/claude-opus-4
    37|hermes config set terminal.backend docker
    38|hermes config set OPENROUTER_API_KEY sk-or-...  # Saves to .env
    39|```
    40|
    41|:::tip
    42|The `hermes config set` command automatically routes values to the right file — API keys are saved to `.env`, everything else to `config.yaml`.
    43|:::
    44|
    45|## Configuration Precedence
    46|
    47|Settings are resolved in this order (highest priority first):
    48|
    49|1. **CLI arguments** — e.g., `hermes chat --model anthropic/claude-sonnet-4` (per-invocation override)
    50|2. **`~/.hermes/config.yaml`** — the primary config file for all non-secret settings
    51|3. **`~/.hermes/.env`** — fallback for env vars; **required** for secrets (API keys, tokens, passwords)
    52|4. **Built-in defaults** — hardcoded safe defaults when nothing else is set
    53|
    54|:::info Rule of Thumb
    55|Secrets (API keys, bot tokens, passwords) go in `.env`. Everything else (model, terminal backend, compression settings, memory limits, toolsets) goes in `config.yaml`. When both are set, `config.yaml` wins for non-secret settings.
    56|:::
    57|
    58|## Environment Variable Substitution
    59|
    60|You can reference environment variables in `config.yaml` using `${VAR_NAME}` syntax:
    61|
    62|```yaml
    63|auxiliary:
    64|  vision:
    65|    api_key: ${GOOGLE_API_KEY}
    66|    base_url: ${CUSTOM_VISION_URL}
    67|
    68|delegation:
    69|  api_key: ${DELEGATION_KEY}
    70|```
    71|
    72|Multiple references in a single value work: `url: "${HOST}:${PORT}"`. If a referenced variable is not set, the placeholder is kept verbatim (`${UNDEFINED_VAR}` stays as-is). Only the `${VAR}` syntax is supported — bare `$VAR` is not expanded.
    73|
    74|For AI provider setup (OpenRouter, Anthropic, Copilot, custom endpoints, self-hosted LLMs, fallback models, etc.), see [AI Providers](/docs/integrations/providers).
    75|
    76|### Provider Timeouts
    77|
    78|You can set `providers.<id>.request_timeout_seconds` for a provider-wide request timeout, plus `providers.<id>.models.<model>.timeout_seconds` for a model-specific override. Applies to the primary turn client on every transport (OpenAI-wire, native Anthropic, Anthropic-compatible), the fallback chain, rebuilds after credential rotation, and (for OpenAI-wire) the per-request timeout kwarg — so the configured value wins over the legacy `HERMES_API_TIMEOUT` env var.
    79|
    80|You can also set `providers.<id>.stale_timeout_seconds` for the non-streaming stale-call detector, plus `providers.<id>.models.<model>.stale_timeout_seconds` for a model-specific override. This wins over the legacy `HERMES_API_CALL_STALE_TIMEOUT` env var.
    81|
    82|Leaving these unset keeps the legacy defaults (`HERMES_API_TIMEOUT=1800`s, `HERMES_API_CALL_STALE_TIMEOUT=300`s, native Anthropic 900s). Not currently wired for AWS Bedrock (both `bedrock_converse` and AnthropicBedrock SDK paths use boto3 with its own timeout configuration). See the commented example in [`cli-config.yaml.example`](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/blob/main/cli-config.yaml.example).
    83|
    84|## Terminal Backend Configuration
    85|
    86|Hermes supports seven terminal backends. Each determines where the agent's shell commands actually execute — your local machine, a Docker container, a remote server via SSH, a Modal cloud sandbox (direct or via the Nous-managed gateway), a Daytona workspace, a Vercel Sandbox, or a Singularity/Apptainer container.
    87|
    88|```yaml
    89|terminal:
    90|  backend: local    # local | docker | ssh | modal | daytona | vercel_sandbox | singularity
    91|  cwd: "."          # Gateway/cron working directory (CLI always uses launch dir)
    92|  timeout: 180      # Per-command timeout in seconds
    93|  env_passthrough: []  # Env var names to forward to sandboxed execution (terminal + execute_code)
    94|  singularity_image: "docker://nikolaik/python-nodejs:python3.11-nodejs20"  # Container image for Singularity backend
    95|  modal_image: "nikolaik/python-nodejs:python3.11-nodejs20"                 # Container image for Modal backend
    96|  daytona_image: "nikolaik/python-nodejs:python3.11-nodejs20"               # Container image for Daytona backend
    97|```
    98|
    99|For cloud sandboxes such as Modal, Daytona, and Vercel Sandbox, `container_persistent: true` means Hermes will try to preserve filesystem state across sandbox recreation. It does not promise that the same live sandbox, PID space, or background processes will still be running later.
   100|
   101|### Backend Overview
   102|
   103|| Backend | Where commands run | Isolation | Best for |
   104||---------|-------------------|-----------|----------|
   105|| **local** | Your machine directly | None | Development, personal use |
   106|| **docker** | Single persistent Docker container (shared across session, `/new`, subagents) | Full (namespaces, cap-drop) | Safe sandboxing, CI/CD |
   107|| **ssh** | Remote server via SSH | Network boundary | Remote dev, powerful hardware |
   108|| **modal** | Modal cloud sandbox | Full (cloud VM) | Ephemeral cloud compute, evals |
   109|| **daytona** | Daytona workspace | Full (cloud container) | Managed cloud dev environments |
   110|| **vercel_sandbox** | Vercel Sandbox | Full (cloud microVM) | Cloud execution with snapshot-backed filesystem persistence |
   111|| **singularity** | Singularity/Apptainer container | Namespaces (--containall) | HPC clusters, shared machines |
   112|
   113|### Local Backend
   114|
   115|The default. Commands run directly on your machine with no isolation. No special setup required.
   116|
   117|```yaml
   118|terminal:
   119|  backend: local
   120|```

## Model bağlamak / değiştirmek için seçenekler

### 1) En kolay yol: interaktif
```bash
hermes model
```
Buradan:
- ana modeli seçersin
- istersen **auxiliary** modelleri de ayarlarsın

### 2) Direkt config üzerinden
Örnek:
```bash
hermes config set model anthropic/claude-sonnet-4
```

Başka örnekler:
```bash
hermes config set model openai/gpt-4o
hermes config set model google/gemini-2.5-flash
```

### 3) API key gerekiyorsa
Anahtarları `.env` içine koyarsın:
```bash
hermes config set OPENROUTER_API_KEY sk-...
```

## Önemli not
Bu makinede `hermes` komutu şu an `PATH` içinde görünmüyor. Yani önce Hermes CLI erişimini düzeltmen gerekebilir. O durumda:
- repo içindeki wrapper ile çalıştırma,
- ya da kurulumu tamamlama

gerekir.

Tamam — **Gemini için ana modeli ayarladım**:

- `provider: gemini`
- `default: gemini-2.5-flash`

Ama önemli nokta: şu anda bu ortamda **Google API key yok**.

## Devam etmek için
Birini eklemelisin:

- `GOOGLE_API_KEY=...`
- veya `GEMINI_API_KEY=...`

Bunu genelde `~/.hermes/.env` içine koyuyoruz.

## Seçenekler
- **API key ile Gemini AI Studio**: mevcut ayar buna göre hazır
- **Google OAuth ile Gemini CLI** istiyorsan: provider’ı `google-gemini-cli` yaparız

İstersen bir sonraki mesajda sana:
1. `~/.hermes/.env` dosyasına ne yazacağını,
2. ya da OAuth yolunu
